name: Sol Yaged
abstract: Sol Yaged (December 8, 1922 – May 11, 2019) was an American jazz clarinetist who was strongly influenced by Benny Goodman.

interviews.title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Jazz Concert in Memory of Sidney Bechet
Standard Time with Michael Feinstein: Happy with the Blues: The Music of Harold Arlen

quotes.text: Right. Carnegie Hall. He always played that, wherever he played. It never failed. He had to play that. People asked for it, and he always had to close his show with “Sing Sing Sing.” Can you imagine playing that 5, 6, 7, 8 times a day and night? He played it at the Paramount Theater. And one time, I’ll never forget, Monk, he was playing at the Paramount Theater in the Pennsylvania Hotel. He was doing a radio broadcast called the Camel Caravan, he was recording, I don’t know when he had time. Gene Krupa told me he had six sets of drums in the city, and Lionel Hampton told me he had six sets of vibraphones, one at the Pennsylvania, one at the recording studio, one at the radio station, one at the Paramount Hotel, he had his drums there, a set of drums there. So it was the Pennsylvania Hotel, the Paramount Theater, CBS right here on 53rd street, you know where I’m talking about Monk? That’s where I saw Benny Goodman for the first time, coming out of the stage door, in I think it was 1937. I couldn’t believe how tall he was. © Fillius Jazz Archive -4-
quotes.caption: Recalling first seeing Benny Goodman in 1937
quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

quotes.text: Yeah, I was just another musician in the band, but it was a picture I made many years ago called “Carnegie Hall,” right. How’d you know that?
quotes.caption: Mentions a picture called 'Carnegie Hall'
quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

quotes.text: They played “Memories of You” in the Benny Goodman movie when the picture opened up, and then they played it again at the end of the movie, and they played it again during the Carnegie Hall scene.
quotes.caption: Recalling 'Memories of You' in a Benny Goodman movie scene
quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: we went up to Boston for two weeks and we played at Mrs. Donahue’s place... he and I had a little falling out because he had Sol Yaged on clarinet

about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Hawkins, that's right, and Sol Yaged and everybody working there, Woody Herman with a small band, with a big band.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Bennett
about_quotes.evidence: I got to meet Sol a few times... I got to sit in with Sol where he was playing. So we spent an evening playing together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Bennett interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 16, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] They had Sol Yaged down there for a while. Sol Yaged, yeah. [INTERVIEWEE] He was down there for a while.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: Sol was the perfect choice, and a very easy guy to work with, so he gave me several weeks of just basic lessons
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
